reasoning: The search results provide a comprehensive timeline of events through September 2025. In April 2024, the Bulgarian parliament tasked its energy minister with renegotiating the 13-year gas deal with BOTAŞ due to its severe financial toll. Throughout mid-2024 and 2025, Bulgarian officials, including Energy Minister Zhecho Stankov, consistently maintained that the agreement lacks a termination clause and that unliterally terminating it would incur a penalty of over BGN 4 billion. By May 2025, officials reiterated that the contract could not simply be terminated. In July 2025 (after the resolution date), prosecutors launched an investigation into the initial signing of the deal, and BOTAŞ representatives were in Sofia for further renegotiation talks. In September 2025, officials were still decrying the agreement as active, noting that it was decapitalizing Bulgargaz while BOTAŞ continued to insist on the full contractual payments. Since both parties treated the agreement as fully active and in renegotiation (rather than cancelled/terminated) throughout and past the July 1, 2025 deadline, the question firmly resolves as No.
